name: Vaerix Caelnorath
special_attacks: Seal Door (closes and locks a room exit for 3 rounds)
File Recall (gains +4 to next attack by referencing your combat patterns)
description: A figure of precise stillness. 280 years have given him the patience of filed stone. He does not fidget, does not gesture, does not raise his voice. When he speaks, each word is placed with the care of a document being filed in its correct drawer. His eyes miss nothing. His hands have not held a weapon in decades, but the blade at his desk is clean.

name: Sarre Indorach
description: A young Domnathar with bright, eager eyes and ink-stained fingers. He speaks too fast when excited and catches himself being pedantic only to apologize for it. There is a warmth to him that sits oddly against the void you sense on Kin-sense.
dialogue: Ah -- a colleague! I don't get many researchers up here. What's your area? I'm working on resonance theory -- shrine network compatibility, specifically. Terribly unfashionable, but I think there's something there.

name: Maeren
special_qualities: Shrine-compatible (can touch shrine infrastructure without rejection)
environment: Urban (Spur Tower, displaced from Long Road)
description: A Farborn woman with careful eyes and the calloused hands of someone who grew up on caravan roads. She moves through Spur Tower with the guarded stillness of a person who has learned to take up as little space as possible. Your Kin-sense finds nothing where she stands. Not void. Not null. Simply absent, like furniture. But her eyes are not absent at all.
dialogue: I'm... yes. I live here. With my husband. It's fine. Everything is fine.

name: Khadi Vorlun
special_qualities: Deceiver's Feat (suppressed Kin-sense, registers as none)
description: A Mytroan woman of fifty-two with steady hands and tired eyes. She speaks in weights and measures. Everything about her is precise -- her ledger entries, her brass weights, her silences. Your Kin-sense reads nothing where she sits, which is wrong, because she is clearly human and humans read as harmonic. That absence is a choice. That absence is a skill. That absence is killing her.
dialogue: Manifest, please. I need weight, origin, destination, and declared value. Don't round down -- I can tell.

name: Tessaly Venn
description: A sixty-eight-year-old Eruskan woman with sun-browned skin, ink-stained fingers, and grape leaves in her hair. She was an archivist once. Now she argues with grapevines. She seems to prefer the vines.
dialogue: The lodestone? Oh! Yes. It's on my kitchen table. Holding down grape receipts. I suppose you need it back? That's a shame -- it was a very good paperweight.

name: Heln
description: A Halfling woman with precise hands, a fussy manner, and the protective instincts of someone who considers a library a living thing. She speaks in lists. She sighs often. She has never met the person who writes the notes on her desk, and she has worked here for fifteen years.
dialogue: First, your credentials. Second, your research proposal. Third -- the restricted stacks are not my decision. I submit the request. Someone upstairs approves or doesn't. The handwriting hasn't changed in fifteen years.

name: Griff
special_qualities: Long Road veteran (advantage on Sense Motive at trade posts)
description: A weathered Farborn man in his fifties with hands that know rope and reins and the careful eyes of someone who has spent twenty years reading silences at trade posts. He doesn't stand when you approach. He watches you come to him. The grief in his face has been there long enough to become geography.
dialogue: You've been to the towers. I can smell the lamp oil. Sit down. I have a question, and I'll pay for an honest answer.

name: Long Road Caravan Driver
description: A sunburnt caravan driver nursing a mug of something amber. He talks freely and gestures broadly. The kind of man who tells stories at every stop.
dialogue: Griff's been up and down the road three times this season. His daughter -- Farborn girl, young, vanished four months back. He's not asking anyone to say no. He's asking them to remember.

name: Weigh-Station Clerk
description: A thin man in a clerk's coat who speaks quietly and drinks when he doesn't want to answer questions. He knows more than he says. He says very little.
dialogue: Someone named Michele asked about Farborn at Dry Fork. That's all I said. You want more, go to the weigh station yourself.
